name: MFS Communications Company, Inc.
description: MFS Communications Company, Inc., originally known as Metropolitan Fiber Systems, was a pioneering competitive local exchange carrier based in the United States. Founded in 1987, the company specialized in owning and operating local network access facilities in major cities across the U.S. and Europe. MFS played a significant role in the telecommunications industry by challenging traditional local telephone monopolies. The company was publicly traded on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol MFST until it was acquired by WorldCom (later part of Verizon Business) in 1996, marking a major consolidation in the telecommunications sector.
